theme.title: Mixed / Stock-Picker's Market
theme.summary: Utilities (+1.91%) and Materials (+1.44%) led the market today, while Real Estate (-0.58%) and Consumer Discretionary (-0.36%) brought up the rear. That split lines up with a rotation out of growth: growth-oriented sectors averaged -0.06% versus +0.70% for defensives and +0.53% for value and cyclicals, suggesting money moved from high-multiple names into steadier, cash-generative businesses. Major indices moved in a tighter band — S&P 500 +0.22%, Dow +0.34%, Nasdaq -0.07% — without one clearly dominating. Small caps underperformed as well, with the Russell 2000 at -0.64%, hinting the move favored large, established balance sheets over smaller, more rate-sensitive companies. The VIX fell 1.41% to 16.81, which reads as an orderly rotation rather than panic-driven selling. Gold added 0.41% to $4,152.60, crude oil was little changed at $86.21, and bitcoin slipped 0.48% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
